questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes NITI Aayog from the Planning Commission it replaced?
options: NITI Aayog has constitutional status and can allocate funds to states directly
NITI Aayog is a policy advisory body with no fund-disbursement authority, unlike the Planning Commission
NITI Aayog focuses exclusively on rural development, while the Planning Commission covered all sectors
NITI Aayog publishes Five-Year Plans under a different name to align with state plans
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is a think-tank and advisory body. It cannot allocate plan funds to states — that power moved to the Finance Commission and direct transfers. The Planning Commission had real resource-allocation authority.

questions.question: If India's fiscal deficit for a year is ₹15 lakh crore and interest payments are ₹9 lakh crore, the primary deficit is:
questions.explanation: Primary deficit = fiscal deficit − interest payments = 15 − 9 = 6 lakh crore. It shows how much the government is borrowing for current and capital expenditure excluding legacy interest burden.

questions.question: Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) differ in that:
options: CRR is maintained with RBI in cash; SLR is maintained by banks in liquid assets like gold and approved securities
CRR applies to scheduled commercial banks only; SLR applies to NBFCs and co-operative banks
CRR funds earn interest from RBI; SLR funds are non-interest bearing
SLR controls money supply directly whereas CRR has no direct monetary effect
questions.explanation: CRR is a fraction of net demand and time liabilities held as cash with RBI — it earns no interest. SLR is maintained in liquid assets (gold, T-bills, approved securities) held by the bank itself. Both are monetary tools but work differently.
